data.content:
Implementation plan:

1. Work in a dedicated branch/worktree and bootstrap dependencies with `bun install --frozen-lockfile`.
2. Extend the settings sheet navigation so callers can request the Dispatch tab, not only Repos. Update the header hook/types to carry that section through cleanly.
3. Thread an `onOpenSettingsDispatch` callback into `StaleBeatGroomingDialog` from the app header. When invoked, close the stale-beats dialog and open Settings on Dispatch.
4. Replace the raw `defaultError` rendering in the stale-beats dialog with a compact neutral helper. Copy should say that Foolery can remember the stale-beat review model via model dispatch, while manual selection in the modal remains fine for one-off reviews. Add an icon button/text button to open Dispatch Settings.
5. Keep review enablement unchanged: a selected explicit model should still allow Review even when no remembered dispatch model exists.
6. Add focused component/unit coverage if there is an existing test harness for these components; otherwise rely on lint/typecheck plus a browser smoke check of the modal.
7. Run `bun run lint`, `bunx tsc --noEmit`, `bun run test`, and `bun run build`; fix any failures before integration.

Complexity: small UI plumbing change across the stale-beats dialog and settings sheet/header path.

Risks: avoid leaking raw configured-resource failure text into the modal, avoid making Settings always open Repos, and avoid disabling the one-off Review path when a model is manually selected.
